channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Florida Insurer Safepoint Reveals 97% Revenue Surge in U.S. IPO Filing
item.description: It could cause a mini slowdown around the week of the SpaceX listing, if companies don’t want to compete for investor attention.” Founded in 2013, Tampa, Florida-based Safepoint is a property and casualty insurer focused on delivering insurance in coastal markets such as Florida and Louisiana, as well as other U.S. initial public offering, disclosing a 96.9% revenue jump last year as a strong run of new listings from Florida-based insurers extends into 2026. Safepoint on Friday filed its paperwork for a U.S.

item.title: 10 Year Study Finds No Real Benefit to Common Knee Surgery
item.description: In the 10-year clinical trial, researchers found that trimming a damaged meniscus — a procedure long believed to relieve pain—offers no real benefit over placebo surgery. A widely performed knee procedure known as partial meniscectomy may not deliver the benefits many patients expect, according to a new study out of the University of Helsinki. The surgery has long been based on the idea that knee pain, especially on the inner side, is caused by a meniscus tear that can be fixed surgically.

item.title: Farmers Insurance in California: HO Rates Up 1.5%; Bundlers Get 22% Discount
item.description: Farmers Insurance announced yesterday that the California Department of Insurance has approved the insurer’s introduction of a new homeowners insurance rating plan, including elements from Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara’s Sustainable Insurance Strategy. According to Farmers, the new homeowners insurance rating plan will adjust rates upwards by a statewide average of 1.5%, but new or existing customers who choose to bundle will generally see their rates drop, in general. “We continue to see encouraging signs that the California insurance marketplace is strengthening and we want to be well-positioned to grow and provide improved coverage offerings to California consumers,” said Behram Dinshaw, president of personal lines for Farmers Insurance, in a media statement.

item.title: Sompo continues Australia team expansion with addition of Everest’s Sophie Morrison
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Antares appoints Sri Ram Pappu as deputy Singapore CEO
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Alt capital sustainability hinges on modeling, transparency, careful product sequencing: GC’s Rousseau
item.description: As alternative capital continues to integrate further into the global reinsurance market, in order for this to be truly sustainable the sector must prioritise better modeling, greater transparency and careful sequencing of product development so that capital can be deployed where it strengthens resilience, according to Laurent Rousseau of Guy Carpenter. “That convergence is not merely about adding supply; it is reshaping the industry’s economics and identity as reinsurers become originators, structurers, and distributors of risk.” He continued: “If this redesign is to be sustainable, the sector must prioritize better modeling, greater transparency around who ultimately bears losses, and careful sequencing of product development so that capital can be deployed where it truly strengthens resilience rather than amplifies systemic fragility.” Meanwhile, Guy Carpenter’s report also highlights how public catastrophe bond issuance continues to reach record levels as more players continue to utilise the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market. Rousseau’s comments come as Guy Carpenter has recently published a new report that examines the accelerating integration of alternative capital into the global reinsurance market and the implications for capacity, pricing, and market structure.

item.title: American Coastal lifting top of reinsurance tower to $1.6bn, adds more aggregate cover: CEO
item.description: The mid-year 2026 reinsurance renewal for American Coastal Insurance Company (AmCoastal) is “effectively complete” its CEO Brad Martz has explained, with the top of the tower lifting to over $1.6bn, while the company has added more aggregate protection as well. A year ago, American Coastal Insurance (AmCoastal) renewed its core catastrophe reinsurance program with first event limit of $1.33 billion at the June 2025 renewal . Martz explained, “Third, we have moved our lower layers to an all-perils basis that will allow us to non-renew the January 1st all other perils catastrophe reinsurance program next year, while maintaining robust protection against potential non-hurricane cat events.” Adding, “Lastly, we have more aggregate protection against frequency and severity resulting from a potentially active hurricane season.”

item.title: IAG eyes GWP of over $25bn by 2030 in new growth plan
item.description: At its Investor Day in Sydney, IAG has unveiled a refreshed strategy and new long-term financial targets under its Ambition 2030 plan, which aims to grow the group’s customer base to more than 11 million and lift gross written premium (GWP) above AU$25 billion. Over the past five years, we have delivered on our targets and laid solid foundations for our new Ambition 2030 goals. Having successfully delivered on targets set in 2021, IAG said that it is well-positioned to deliver Ambition 2030 and four strategic priorities: Customer Obsession; Insurance Excellence; Future Fit Operations; and Exceptional People.

item.title: Octave Specialty Group sees $13m net income and 92% revenue growth in Q1’26
item.description: Global specialty insurance firm, Octave Specialty Group, formerly known as Ambac Financial Group, announced its financial results for the first quarter of 2026, reporting net income to shareholders of $13 million, and total revenue growth of 92%. Octave also maintained a robust 42% organic revenue growth rate. Our core Insurance Distribution business delivered 92% revenue growth, 42% organic growth, the rest from our recent ArmadaCare acquisition.

item.title: Price softening to persist into 2027 without significant industry cat loss: JP Morgan
item.description: Unless there is a significantly large industry catastrophe loss, reinsurance pricing will continue to soften into 2027, JP Morgan analysts highlight in a most recent Love Actuary report. Using the historic market shares of losses from their Break in Case of Emergency database, JP Morgan calculated that “to wipe out the underwriting profits for the largest European reinsurers, the industry loss would need to be ~$120bn on top of a normal catastrophe load for the year. The acceleration of reinsurance price softening is evident in 2026, a trend unlikely to stop as mid-year renewals near, driven by robust surplus capital levels and impressive first-quarter 2026 earnings.

item.title: People Moves: Brown & Riding Adds Armstrong to Private Client Practice
item.description: Brown & Riding announced Susannah Armstrong to its private client practice within the firm’s underwriting & select accounts division, specializing in high-net-worth personal lines. With over 10 years of industry experience across sales, service, and marketing in the insurance space, Armstrong brings a strong background in client engagement, communications, and brand development to her new role. She is known for her knowledge and expertise in the private client sector and has built her career on adaptability, integrity, and always working to find the right fit for her retailers’ needs.

item.title: Illinois Company Sued Over Alleged Home Repair Scam
item.description: Raoul’s lawsuit alleges Premium Home Service’s scheme lures consumers in with local phone numbers for what consumers think are local home repair businesses; the defendants allegedly maintained over 7,600 telephone numbers in over 250 area codes across the U.S. Well over 100,000 consumers nationwide have been deceived into calling and purchasing home repair services and memberships since at least 2018. GDR LLC, doing business as Premium Home Service, and its Chief Executive Officer Yosef Bernath, alleging the business created thousands of online business profiles for home repair companies that did not exist.

item.title: Maryland Announces $2.5 Billion Settlement Over Baltimore Bridge Collapse
item.description: The state of Maryland has finalized a $2.5 billion settlement with Grace Ocean Private Limited and Synergy Marine Pte Ltd., the owner and operator of the cargo ship Dali, over the ship’s March 26, 2024 allision that caused the collapse of the Baltimore’s Key Bridge and killed six workers. Single Loose Wire Led to Blackout That Caused Dali Crash Into Baltimore Bridge “The collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge sent shockwaves through Maryland and caused damages on a scale this State had never seen,” said Brown. US Brings Criminal Charges Over Collapse of Baltimore Bridge The three have been charged with conspiracy, willfully failing to immediately inform the U.S.

item.title: Brown & Brown targets 25% submission automation with AI while ruling out job cuts
item.description: The post Brown & Brown targets 25% submission automation with AI while ruling out job cuts appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Brown & Brown outlined an expanded AI strategy during its Q1 2026 earnings call, saying the brokerage expects artificial intelligence to automate more than 25% of submission workflows across many wholesale and program businesses, while emphasizing that the technology is intended to augment employees rather than replace them. Brown & Brown also highlighted several additional AI use cases now in production, including: AI-powered policy checking agents that automate proposal comparisons and policy reviews Systems capable of summarizing complex insurance policies for customers A proprietary billing platform that interfaces with carrier portals to automate billing extraction and validation workflows The company said the billing automation platform alone is saving more than 50,000 hours annually.

item.title: Sixfold launches Narrative AI capability for Life & Health underwriting
item.description: Sixfold has introduced Narrative, a new AI-powered capability designed to help Life & Health insurers accelerate underwriting reviews by automatically generating structured case summaries from medical and underwriting documents. The launch addresses one of the biggest operational bottlenecks in Life & Health underwriting: manual document review. While accelerated underwriting has improved straight-through processing, a large majority of cases still require human review, forcing underwriters to sift through electronic health records, attending physician statements, lab results, and other medical files to identify relevant risk information.

item.title: Insurance AI Requires Specialized Guardrails
item.description: Generic AI safety tools can't address insurance's unique risks; specialized guardrails are essential for responsible deployment. Insurance-specific guardrails, which control all aspects of the interaction of artificial intelligence, from input validation to output verification, are a necessity. For the insurance industry, where decisions have significant consequences, general-purpose safety controls aren't enough to ensure the safe deployment of large language models.

item.title: P&C Insurance's AI Problem Isn't What You Think
item.description: That imbalance is the first signal of what Capgemini identifies in the 19th edition of its 2026 World Property and Casualty Insurance Report as an "architecture mismatch." This is a structural gap that runs deeper than the technology stack, and that no amount of additional AI investment will close on its own. Yet 42% of insurers track no AI metrics at all, which means they have no way to validate what works, no playbook to scale it, and no mechanism to stop what doesn't work. These three dimensions are entangled, which is precisely what makes the conventional response insufficient.

item.title: Northern Re Expands Use of Ultrassure for Contract Workflows
item.description: (“Ultrassure”) today announced that Northern Reinsurance (“Northern Re”) has expanded its use of Ultrassure’s platform from an initial pilot to production deployment for contract workflows across its reinsurance operations. During the pilot, Northern Re and Ultrassure worked together to refine the platform’s fit for day-to-day contract work, with a focus on usability, responsiveness to feedback, and operational readiness. The production rollout reflects Northern Re’s decision to move the platform into active use after testing it in live workflows and assessing how it could support contract operations with greater consistency, efficiency, and control.

item.title: Markel Canada partners with Hyperexponential
item.description: As part of its collaboration with hyperexponential, Markel Canada has launched a purpose-built Environmental rating capability on the hx platform, enabling a more streamlined, digital experience. By pulling data directly into the rating workflow, we’re cutting friction from everyday decisions and letting our teams focus on what matters most – building trusted relationships with our broker partners, understanding risk and delivering consistent outcomes for clients.” – Cliff Laidlaw, Senior Vice President, Underwriting at Markel Canada. Markel International announced its partnership with Hyperexponential to modernize rating, underwriting workflows and integration architecture across its Canadian business.

item.title: Huntress and Acrisure launch no-deductible cyber insurance program
item.description: Huntress and Acrisure launched a cyber insurance program offering eligible businesses access to Cyber or Tech E&O coverage with no deductible and a streamlined application process. The program is designed for Huntress customers and partners using qualifying managed EDR and identity threat detection products, with coverage placed through Acrisure. The program aims to simplify cyber insurance purchasing as businesses face rising AI-driven cyber threats and increasingly complex underwriting requirements.

item.title: ‘Designed To Last’: American Family Launches Reality Competition Series on Hul
item.description: American Family Insurance has launched a branded reality competition series, “Designed To Last,” a first-of-its-kind show that reimagines how to think about protecting homes and families against increasing climate risk. “At American Family Insurance, our mission is to inspire, protect and restore dreams — and the ‘Designed To Last’ series brings that commitment to life by recognizing the home is where dreams begin.” “The next evolution of brand storytelling is about delivering value, not just visibility,” said John Campbell, SVP, entertainment and streaming solutions at Disney Advertising. “Together with American Family Insurance, ‘Designed To Last’ builds a story that combines compelling entertainment with practical insights to deliver experience more relevant, more useful, and ultimately more impactful for audiences.” Hosted by Maria Menounos, the series follows three teams of architects, engineers and inventors as they compete in 10-hour build challenges, modifying tiny homes to withstand specific environmental hazards.

item.title: How Your ORSA Can Be Retooled for a Competitive Advantage
item.description: This article is the second part of a two-part article series that began in February with the article, " 10 Dos and Don'ts of a Smart ORSA Report ." Your primary responsibility is to ensure the company’s success, including financial solvency, with the board, owners/shareholders, policyholders and employees holding you accountable. They are much less concerned about whether the organization is achieving its potential.” (emphasis added) Instead of an “enterprise list management” approach or mindset that breeds this sentiment, today’s article provides a blueprint for how carrier executives can use ORSA to their advantage. As a carrier executive, you probably don’t get excited at the thought of complying with regulations like the Own Risk Solvency Assessment, commonly known as ORSA.

item.title: Swiss Re elevates Australia, New Zealand CEO Trent Thomson to global specialty head
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Russell Hails to lead Gallagher Re’s APAC MGA solutions team
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA fines and bans Frank Breuer for serious misconduct in pension transfer advice
item.description: Therese Chambers, joint executive director of enforcement and market oversight, said: 'Mr Breuer sought to evade paying compensation due to customers as a result of his own bad pension advice and feathered his own nest in the process, stripping substantial assets from his firm. The FCA has banned Frank Breuer from working in UK financial services and fined him £755,000 for repeatedly acting without integrity and putting customers at risk for personal financial gain. Mr Breuer was the joint owner and sole director of Bluesky Wealth Management Limited (Bluesky), which provided advice on investments and pensions.

item.title: European AI Funding Is Growing. Will That Boost The Region’s Startup Scene?
item.description: A growing percentage of European venture funding in 2026 was AI-driven. The uptick in artificial intelligence investment has coincided with an overall gain in startup funding in the region the last past quarters. All told, roughly half of European venture funding in 2026 to date has been in AI-related companies, Crunchbase data shows.

item.title: Nationwide Mutual now targets up to $325m of reinsurance from Aquila Re I 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: Nationwide Mutual Insurance Company is aiming to upsize its new catastrophe bond, with the revised target now being to secure up to $325 million in multi-peril reinsurance protection through its new Aquila Re I Ltd. The two tranches of Aquila Re I Series 2026-1 cat bond notes on offer will protect Nationwide Mutual against losses from the perils of US named storm, earthquake, severe thunderstorm, winter storm, wildfire, meteorite impact, volcanic eruption, the same range of perils as its previous two cat bonds under the structure. Nationwide Mutual made its return to the catastrophe bond market in April, with this new Aquila Re I deal set to become the eleventh Nationwide sponsored catastrophe bond issuance that we have listed in our extensive Deal Directory .

item.title: W. R. Berkley cedes more premium again to Lifson Re sidecar, earns more fees in Q1 2026
item.description: Berkley Corporation, the US-headquartered insurance holding company, once again reported higher premiums being ceded to its Lifson Re collateralized reinsurance sidecar for Q1 2026, while the company also recognised more fee income being earned from the structure. Berkley capitalised the Lifson Re sidecar to the tune of $418 million for 2025, which marked the vehicle’s largest capital raise at the time. Berkley has been ceding higher amounts of premium to the sidecar as it utilises the vehicle to help manage risk, while also generating returns for the company.

item.title: 27 years of Artemis, nearly $220bn of cat bonds and ILS tracked in our Deal Directory
item.description: Today is the 27th anniversary of the official launch of Artemis, so as usual for our celebratory official-birthday article (which is on the date for once) we wanted to highlight that since beginning to track catastrophe bond and insurance-linked security (ILS) issuance activity we’ve analysed and included nearly $220 billion of transaction volume in our extensive Deal Directory . The Artemis Deal Directory was born as a listing of the very earliest catastrophe bonds on a website called the Risk Information & Services Exchange (RSX) back in 1996, then finding its home on Artemis in 1999 when it was decided that the developing catastrophe bond market deserved a dedicated home on the internet. While Artemis was created from late 1998 and launched in 1999, the catastrophe bond Deal Directory was actually already in existence, as we began tracking early insurance-linked securities activity from the very first deals.

item.title: Assurex Global appoints Troy Johnson as SVP for global growth strategy
item.description: Assurex Global, a privately owned commercial insurance, risk management and employee benefits brokerage group, has announced the appointment of Troy Johnson as Senior Vice President, Global Growth Strategy. This strategic promotion empowers Troy to accelerate global revenue growth, unlocking unprecedented opportunities for our Partnership and ensuring our clients receive world-class support anywhere they operate.” Johnson has more than 25 years’ experience in risk and insurance brokerage. “As our Partner Firms rapidly scale their capabilities worldwide, Troy brings the visionary leadership necessary to navigate complex international markets,” commented Dean Hildebrandt, President and Chief Executive Officer of Assurex Global.

item.title: ROE for US P&C insurers reached decade high level in 2025: AM Best
item.description: property & casualty (P&C) insurers reached a decade high level of 14.97% in 2025, while the cost of equity held relatively steady at 8.18%. AM Best continued, “Health insurers’ returns have been steadily declining since the pandemic, due to more significant claims, as well as rising medical and pharmaceutical costs—especially specialty drugs, such as GLP-1s.” Meanwhile, AM Best noted that in the life & annuity (L&A) segment, high interest rates throughout 2024 drove exceptional returns, but as rates began to decline in 2025, slowing new money yields weighed on returns. According to the rating agency, the median return on capital employed for P&C insurers continued its upward three-year trend, reaching a new high of 12.41% in 2025.

item.title: U.S. Treasury and insurance regulators discuss evolving risk oversight
item.description: This meeting provided a forum for state insurance regulators to share insights regarding their oversight of insurers’ risk management and investment practices. “We appreciated the opportunity to meet with Secretary Bessent and share how state insurance regulators are leveraging effective oversight and enhancing risk-mitigation frameworks to promote stable markets and deliver strong outcomes for consumers.” The U.S. Treasury Secretary, Scott Bessent, to discuss the growing intersection of private credit and the insurance sector.

item.title: Family of FSU Mass Shooting Victim Sues OpenAI
item.description: The family of a man killed in a 2025 mass shooting at Florida State University has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI in a U.S. accusing OpenAI of facilitating a mass shooting. “Last year’s mass shooting at Florida State University was a tragedy, but ChatGPT is not responsible for this terrible crime,” OpenAI spokesperson Drew Pusateri said in a statement.

item.title: Brown & Brown Wins Temporary Injunction Against Howden
item.description: Brown & Brown late last week received a measured victory when a Minnesota judge granted the broker a temporary restraining order against rival broker Howden US, who stands accused of taking hundreds of employees from Brown & Brown. According to court records from the Hennepin County (Minn.) District Court, the temporary restraining order bars 16 of Brown & Brown’s former employees, now at Howden, from soliciting customers or recruiting staff. A judge in Massachusetts issued a temporary restraining order in December, Brown & Brown executives said, though Howden’s response to the request placed blame firmly on Brown & Brown, saying the rival treated its former employees poorly and undercompensated them.

item.title: Nearly 7M Cars Have Unfixed Backup Camera Recalls: CARFAX
item.description: roads have unfixed recalls on their backup camera system, which can reduce driver visibility and increase the risk of a crash. Recalls related to backup cameras have risen sharply, up by more than a million since the start of the year, according to CARFAX data. These fixes are free and can help prevent potential accidents.” 10 states with the highest number of unfixed camera recalls include: The vehicle history company says several factors are contributing to the rise in camera-related recalls.

item.title: Antares Global launches Scorpius MGA platform under Ian Morris
item.description: Antares Global has launched Scorpius MGA, a new managing general agent platform designed to unify the group’s MGA operations under a single structure while expanding its distribution capabilities across the UK and selected international markets. Mike van der Straaten, CEO of Antares Global, described the launch as a strategic evolution of the group’s MGA relationships and said the platform would support more effective capital deployment while expanding access to specialist markets. The new platform will be led by CEO Ian Morris and is intended to centralize capacity access, governance, onboarding, and shared operational services for MGA partners.

item.title: Intelligent AI research highlights systemic UK underwriting “Risk Intelligence Gap”
item.description: The post Intelligent AI research highlights systemic UK underwriting “Risk Intelligence Gap” appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Intelligent AI has released new research identifying what it describes as a systemic “Risk Intelligence Gap” across the UK insurance market, with property underwriting decisions frequently being made using incomplete or inaccurate data. Wrong pricing, maybe a hundred thousand.” Anthony Peake, founder and CEO of Intelligent AI, said insurers that solve the underlying data quality problem will be better positioned to improve risk selection, reduce volatility, and accelerate the adoption of agentic AI underwriting capabilities.

item.title: Alliant launches FleetLytics
item.description: Alliant Insurance Services announced the launch of FleetLytics, an analytics software designed to help fleets of every size turn data into improved insurability, safety and revenue outcomes. FleetLytics addresses these challenges by helping fleets leverage the data they already generate to demonstrate safety performance, reduce claims and improve insurability.” – Lisa Paul, Executive Vice President, Alliant Transportation. FleetLytics turns fleet telematics and claims data into actionable insights, enabling fleets to translate real-time mileage, driver behavior and claims trends into better coverage, lower premiums and “measurable return on investment in as little as 30 days.” FleetLytics is compatible with most fleet telematics, camera and in-vehicle technology providers, with a centralized dashboard that consolidates safety, compliance, telematics and claims data into a single, real-time view of fleet activity.

item.title: NFP expands private wealth business in Ireland with Metis acquisition
item.description: NFP acquired Irish financial planning and investment advisory firm Metis Ireland as it continues to expand its private wealth business in Ireland. Founded in 2014 and regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland, Metis Ireland provides financial planning, investment advisory, and wealth structuring services to private clients, executives, and business owners from offices in Limerick and Dublin. Following the deal, Metis Ireland managing director Carl Widger will join NFP as head of Wealth and Financial Planning for NFP in Ireland, reporting to Colm Power, managing director of NFP in Ireland.

item.title: Mercury Insurance backs BurnBot
item.description: Mercury Insurance announced a strategic investment in BurnBot, a wildfire mitigation technology company. Working with BurnBot, we are increasing our understanding of how on-the-ground risk reduction translates into expanded insurance availability and improved affordability for homeowners and communities.” – Victor Joseph, President and Chief Operating Officer of Mercury Insurance. We are excited to support Mercury in building practical pathways that connect real-world risk reduction with insurance outcomes.” – Anukool Lakhina, CEO of BurnBot.

item.title: España será el país europeo de mayor crecimiento en seguros embebidos hasta 2031
item.description: Según el informe ‘Europe Embedded Insurance Market Size & Share Analysis – Growth Trends and Forecast (2026‑2031)’ , elaborado por Mordor Intelligence, el mercado mundial de seguro embebido pasará de mover 13.880 millones de dólares en 2025 a 18.090 millones en 2026, y alcanzará los 68.120 millones en 2031, lo que supone una tasa media de crecimiento anual (CAGR) del 30,37% entre 2026 y 2031. Este crecimiento refleja un cambio estructural en los modelos de comercialización del seguro, que deja de ofrecerse como un producto independiente para integrarse directamente en las plataformas digitales donde surge la necesidad de protección . Uno de los principales motores del crecimiento es la integración del seguro en el checkout digital .

item.title: Grupo Mutua Propietarios presenta una nueva línea para reformas de inmuebles
item.description: Grupo Mutua Propietarios lanza HogarGo , un nuevo negocio destinado a las reformas integrales del hogar. Con esta línea, el grupo da un nuevo paso acompañando a los propietarios en momentos clave de la vida de sus inmuebles, con soluciones integrales interconectadas. La propuesta se basa en la optimización de las reformas, el cumplimiento de los plazos y la excelencia en la calidad del servicio, tanto en lo relativo a procesos como a materiales.

item.title: Mapfre Re emite un bono catástrofe de 200 millones para tormentas en Estados Unidos
item.description: Mapfre Re ha completado, a través de su vehículo de propósito especial Recoletos Re DAC, una transacción de bono catástrofe por importe de 200 millones de dólares para la cobertura de tormentas con nombre en Estados Unidos. La operación introduce por primera vez cobertura indemnizatoria por evento frente a tormentas con nombre en Estados Unidos , integrada en el programa corporativo de compra de reaseguro de Mapfre Re para el grupo. La operación ofrece cobertura indemnizatoria por evento con un periodo de riesgo de tres años, hasta finales de junio de 2029 .

item.title: Family of Florida Mass Shooting Victim Suing OpenAI
item.description: The family of a man killed in a 2025 mass shooting at Florida State University has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI in a U.S. “Last year’s mass shooting at Florida State University was a tragedy, but ChatGPT is not responsible for this terrible crime,” OpenAI spokesperson Drew Pusateri said in a statement. The family of Tiru Chabba filed the lawsuit on Sunday in Florida federal court against the company and the man charged in the shooting, Phoenix Ikner.

item.title: Google Says Hackers Are Pushing Innovation in AI-Enabled Hacking Operations
item.description: John Hultquist, chief analyst at Google Threat Intelligence Group, said the findings likely represent the “tip of the iceberg” in terms of how criminals and state-backed hackers are pushing AI hacking innovation. Hackers from a prominent cybercrime group used artificial intelligence to uncover a previously unknown software flaw and an exploit to take advantage of it for the first time, Alphabet’s Google said on Monday. The report also detailed how hackers are beginning to hand parts of their cyber operations to artificial intelligence, using it to autonomously hunt for software flaws and help build malware.

item.title: Netflix Sued for Allegedly Spying on Children, Addicting Users
item.description: Netflix was sued on Monday by Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, who accused the streaming company of spying on children and other consumers by collecting their data without consent, and designing its platform to be addictive. “Netflix’s endgame is simple and lucrative: get children and families glued to the screen, harvest their data while they are stuck there, and then monetize the data for a handsome profit,” according to Texas’ complaint filed in a state court in Collin County, near Dallas. Texas said Netflix has for years falsely represented to consumers that it did not collect or share user data, when it actually tracked and sold viewers’ habits and preferences to commercial data brokers and advertising technology companies, making billions of dollars a year.

item.title: Envest names Norm Casamento as head of technical motor claims
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Munich Re cut book by 18.5% at 1.4 renewals as market softens
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Sedgwick appoints Keith Lee as executive director for energy and global specialty
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Munich Re pulls back at renewals, sees competition as “still mainly on price”
item.description: Munich Re made a “deliberate reduction in premium volume” at the April reinsurance renewals, systematically opting not to renew business that did not meet expectations on price or terms and conditions, but one signal of encouragement is that the company said it sees high levels of competition in the sector as “still mainly on price.” As a result, Munich Re sees portfolio resilience as “largely maintained”, with the reinsurance company seeing terms, conditions and structures as mostly stable in the softening marketplace. Munich Re has announced its first-quarter performance this morning, reporting a net result of just over EUR 1.7 billion, soundly beating the prior year period result of EUR 1.094 billion. Andrew Buchanan, Chief Financial Officer said, “Munich Re has made an excellent start to 2026 with a Q1 result of €1.7bn.
